items.title: Acronyms Ad Absurdum
items.summary: Daniel’s Acronym Fatigue Series started this train of thought, and everything came flashing back that I thought I would write it out.

items.title: The Uncanny Valley
items.summary: Or, why almost-human is worse than not-human at all.

items.title: BODD - Digital Doppelgänger for your AI
items.summary: Today, we are co-living with some form of Artificial Intelligence (AI), and we cannot ignore this digital realm anymore.

items.title: Boring Wins: Stop Trying to Be Interesting
items.summary: I’ve been obsessed with studying how enterprises actually hold onto accounts. Not just win them. Hold them, grow them, make them impossible to remove.

items.title: Pitch Deck: Keep It Simple, Stupid
items.summary: Innovate the Business, Technology, Culture; Not with the Pitch Deck.

items.title: When Execution Is Cheap, Judgment Becomes Scarce
items.summary: When Stripe was still small, the Collison brothers decided to focus on a handful of their Startup cohort at Y Combinator. They had interest from hundreds of potential customers but chose depth over breadth.

items.title: The Conductor’s Model for Enterprise Go-To-Market
items.summary: Go-to-Market (GTM) mishaps are often coordination failures rather than execution failures. Every smart person on the team is working at their best; marketing is busy, sales is active, and product is shipping. The gong gets hit. And yet, when things settle, and the hard questions get asked, the results rarely match the noise.

items.title: Glue Work in Software Product Design and Development
items.summary: You know how things work, understand customers better than most people on the team, see where the product is headed, and you are called in to calm the never-ending designer-developer feud over how things should be.
